mnemonics.title: DAD Principle
mnemonics.content: Declare (data_type *ptr), Assign Address (ptr = &var), Dereference (*ptr) - Three essential steps for pointer operations

mnemonics.title: Ampersand vs Asterisk
mnemonics.content: '&' Adds Address (gets location), '*' Asks Actual value (retrieves content) - Remember: Ampersand is WHERE, Asterisk is WHAT

mnemonics.title: NULL Safety
mnemonics.content: No Useless Links Left - Always initialize pointers to NULL if not immediately assigned to prevent dangling pointers

mnemonics.title: Pointer Arithmetic
mnemonics.content: Step Size Matters: ptr++ moves by sizeof(data_type) bytes, not 1 byte. Visualize as staircase steps of different heights

acronyms.usage: Steps for pointer usage: Store address, Target variable, Access value, Reference memory locations
Pointer lifecycle: Point to memory, Assign address, Reference value, Transform through arithmetic
Always remember & operator means 'address of' a variable

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a USB drive (pointer) containing a paper note with a locker number (memory address). The locker holds your actual data
Picture a treasure map (*ptr) where X marks the spot (memory address). Digging (dereferencing) reveals the treasure (value)
Visualize an apartment complex. ptr++ moves to next apartment (memory location) based on tenant size (data type)
A blank name tag (NULL pointer) that isn't assigned to any attendee (memory location) yet

analogies.analogy: Like a GPS coordinate - int *ptr doesn't store the destination itself, but the directions to reach it
Similar to using a key (*ptr) to open a locker (&var) - the key isn't valuable itself, but gives access to contents
Like setting a web browser bookmark (ptr = &var) - clicking it later (*ptr) takes you to the saved page (value)
